Scattered trees were downed across a nearly one mile wide swath from the northwest side of Cornell to three miles east of Cornell. Some trees fell on houses and vehicles. A gazebo was also damaged, several garage doors buckled, and some shingles came off several roofs. Windows in a barn were blown out and a corn field was damaged. Photographs indicate downburst damage.

A brief tornado developed along the leading edge of a line of storms and moved into New London before dissipating. The roofs of three houses and one business were partially removed. Dozens of trees were broken or uprooted. The line of storms also produced damaging downburst and inflow winds.

A brief tornado touched down just south of the intersection of County Road 14 and 30th St SE. It tracked northeast and mainly uprooted or broke over 100 trees, most of them at one farmstead. Tornado rated EF0 with maximum wind of 85 mph. Path length was 0.68 mile and maximum width was 25 yards.
